rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 9, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC, CRESCENT HEIGHTS INC, HOWARD S WRIGHT CONSTRUCTION CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. The term "amended" in para. 4 (really para. 3) of the settlement document is ambiguous. Defendant argues that it is a term of art and the exclusion for release only applied to pending lawsuits. The HOA argues that the intent of the parties was to exclude plaintiff's claims in whatever form they took. Defendants' term of art fails. The contract defines amendment as including the filing of a cross-action, and a cross-action is not technically an amendment. Plaintiff is also correct that the HOA lacked the authority to release plaintiffs individual claims. The 3rd amended complaint alleges personal claims that the HOA could not settle per CCP 1368.3. =(302/LMG)

Motion continued to September 20, 2011. Defendant's counsel must provide a declaration under penalty of perjury as to why he delayed in bringing this motion sooner and the facts showing excusable neglect. The declaration must be filed and served by September 12, 2011 and a courtesy copy (with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date) provided to department 302. Plaintiff may file and serve its supplemental reply (with a courtesy copy to department 302) by September 15, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD LA FORCE et al VS. WAYNE CORCORAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Charles Schwab & Co. Inc., A Corporation'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 9, 2011, line 15. Defendant Charles Schwab & Co. Inc., A Corporation'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiff has adequately pled facts that show that Corcoran was clothed with apparent authority to make representation about products carried on the defendant Schwab's platform, including Transtar. Whether or not this is enough for a reasonable person to believe that there was an agency relationship is a question of fact that is not appropriate for determination on a demurrer. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SUSANNA LORANT VS. GAEL ALVARADO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 9, 2011, line 9. DEFENDANT GAEL ALVARADO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The 30-day notice is fatally defective. Plaintiff does not state just cause for the eviction or provide any facts showing how the premises is exempt from the just cause requirement. Plaintiff's reliance on Kaufman is misplaced. It only applied to a move-out agreement to settle litigation. =(302/LMG)
